population: Sepsis-3 patients admitted to ICU with lactate >2 mmol/L at ICU admission
method: Prognostic performance assessment; Estimated Mortality Ratio (EMR) using Swedish 2016 SAPS-3 calibration
notes: Patients divided into survivors (alive >30 days after ICU admission) and non-survivors (dead within 30 days). Study also aimed to find a simpler model with fewer parameters but maintained predictive value.

notes: Worst recorded values used for SOFA and SAPS-3 calculation. Septic shock defined as vasopressor use. Immunosuppression defined as immunosuppressive treatment or neutropaenia with ANC <1.5x10/L before ICU admission.

predictor: Six-parameter model (age, immunosuppression, CRP, bilirubin, GCS score, body temperature)
outcome: ICU mortality (30-day or in-hospital, not specified)
method: Backward stepwise multivariate logistic regression, ROC analysis
effect_size: OR >1 for age, immunosuppression, CRP, bilirubin; OR <1 for GCS score and body temperature
performance: AUC 0.72 (95% CI 0.69–0.75); internal validation AUC 0.71 (95% CI 0.66–0.76)
notes: No significant difference in predictive strength compared to SAPS-3 (p=0.071); better predictive value than SOFA score (p=0.0019)

outcome: ICU mortality (30-day or in-hospital, not specified)
notes: Better predictive value than SOFA score (p<0.001)

predictor: Six-parameter model (age, immunosuppression, GCS score, body temperature, CRP level, bilirubin level)
effect_size: Higher risk with: advanced age, immunosuppression, low GCS, low body temperature, increased CRP, increased bilirubin
notes: Model combines measures of systemic inflammation (CRP), immune dysregulation (normo- or hypothermia during sepsis)
Low or normal body temperature associated with increased mortality in sepsis; reflected in SAPS-3
Elevated bilirubin levels at ICU admission identified as early independent predictor of mortality; included in simplified prognostic model
Elevated CRP levels found to be prognostic; included in simplified model; identified as strong independent prognostic marker for 30-day mortality in a separate publication by the same research group
Elevated lactate levels strongly correlated with 30-day mortality in univariate analysis but did not add enough independent predictive power to be included in the simplified model

performance: No significant difference between survivors and non-survivors
notes: WBC showed no significant difference between survivors and non-survivors; not of predictive value in sepsis patients

performance: Lower prognostic value in sepsis than in general ICU population
notes: SAPS-3 has lower prognostic value in sepsis ICU admissions compared to general ICU population
